TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: naizua ti sa Ḍangaḍang, navaik a sema pana sema Tjuruquɫivangerangeraw.
S.PHON: naizua ti sa ɖaŋaɖaŋ, navaik a səma pana səma curuquʎivaŋəraŋəraw.
S.FORM: naizua ti sa drangadrang, navaik a sema pana sema Tjuruquljivangerangeraw.
S.PHON: naizua ti sa ɖaŋaɖaŋ, navaik a səma pana səma ʦuruquʎivaŋəraŋəraw.
S.TRANSL: There was a person called Ḍangaḍang. He went to the river to the Place of Many Rainbows.

S.FORM: ka manu manadip a lepets a pinanadipan.
S.PHON: ka manu manadip a ɭəpəʦ a pinanadipan.
S.FORM: ka manu manadip a lepec a pinanadipan.
S.PHON: ka manu manadip a ɭəpəʦ a pinanadipan.
S.TRANSL: When he fished with a hook, he caught a mourning head-cloth.

S.FORM: saka mumaɫ a manadip a kavakavanga.
S.PHON: saka mumaʎ a manadip a kavakavaŋa.
S.FORM: saka mumalj a manadip a kavakavanga.
S.PHON: saka mumaʎ a manadip a kavakavaŋa.
S.TRANSL: The next time he caught a robe.

S.FORM: saka maɫia manadip uta, saka tsautsau anga.
S.PHON: saka maʎia manadip uta, saka ʦauʦau aŋa.
S.FORM: saka maljia manadip uta, saka caucau anga.
S.PHON: saka maʎia manadip uta, saka ʦauʦau aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Next day he fished again, and this time it was a person.

S.FORM: sa "nakuya tsu.
S.PHON: sa "nakuja ʦu.
S.FORM: sa "nakuya cu.
S.PHON: sa "nakuja ʦu.

S.FORM: kimaya siqazaɫ; kimaya ken a uri matsay." aya, sa vaik anga sema umaq.
S.PHON: kimaja siqazaʎ; kimaja kən a uri maʦaj." aja, sa vaik aŋa səma umaq.
S.FORM: kimaya siqazalj; kimaya ken a uri macay." aya, sa vaik anga sema umaq.
S.PHON: kimaja siqazaʎ; kimaja kən a uri maʦaj." aja, sa vaik aŋa səma umaq.
S.TRANSL: There’s something frightful about it. Perhaps I’m going to die,” he said, and went home.

S.FORM: ka mangtjez i umaq, "palisi anga izua.
S.PHON: ka maŋcəz i umaq, "paɭisi aŋa izua.
S.FORM: ka mangtjez i umaq, "palisi anga izua.
S.PHON: ka maŋʦəz i umaq, "paɭisi aŋa izua.
S.TRANSL: When he got home, the villagers said: “That’s taboo.

S.FORM: mayanga a sema zua penanadip, a umaq nua tsemas," ayanga tamaqaɫan.
S.PHON: majaŋa a səma zua pənanadip, a umaq nua ʦəmas," ajaŋa tamaqaʎan.
S.FORM: mayanga a sema zua penanadip, a umaq nua cemas," ayanga tamaqaljan.
S.PHON: majaŋa a səma zua pənanadip, a umaq nua ʦəmas," ajaŋa tamaqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: Don’t go there to fish; it’s the home of a god.”

S.FORM: saka ini anga ka sema zua; marekutj anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: saka ini aŋa ka səma zua; marəkuc aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: saka ini anga ka sema zua; marekutj anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: saka ini aŋa ka səma zua; marəkuʦ aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: So he didn’t go there any more; he was too frightened.

S.FORM: naizua ti Qeɫung kati sa Pangil a sema zua semenasenay a maresudjusudju.
S.PHON: naizua ti qəʎuŋ kati sa paŋiɭ a səma zua səmənasənaj a marəsuɉusuɉu.
S.FORM: naizua ti Qeljung kati sa Pangil a sema zua semenasenay a maresudjusudju.
S.PHON: naizua ti qəʎuŋ kati sa paŋiɭ a səma zua səmənasənaj a marəsuɉusuɉu.
S.TRANSL: There were Qeɫung and Pangil who went there singing and courting.

S.FORM: matsay ti sa Qeɫung, ka mangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: maʦaj ti sa qəʎuŋ, ka maŋcəz i umaq.
S.FORM: macay ti sa Qeljung, ka mangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: maʦaj ti sa qəʎuŋ, ka maŋʦəz i umaq.
S.TRANSL: Qeɫung died when he got home.

S.FORM: sa nekanga nu ɫiadet a sema zua.
S.PHON: sa nəkaŋa nu ʎiadət a səma zua.
S.FORM: sa nekanga nu ljiadet a sema zua.
S.PHON: sa nəkaŋa nu ʎiadət a səma zua.
S.TRANSL: So noone else went near there.

S.FORM: amin a kai.
S.PHON: amin a kai.
S.FORM: amin a kai.
S.PHON: amin a kai.
